summary:
During acid etching for dentin preparation, it removes the smear layer, exposes collagen fibers, and opens dentinal tubules; increasing surface tension is not a typical effect. \boxed{D}

The summary captures that acid-etching affects removing the smear layer, exposing collagen, opening tubules but explicitly states which one doesn't apply among common knowledge. It also directly addresses why 'increasing surface tension' stands as an exception based upon standard procedures described by Agent2 initially.
